summary: The run sheet is ready for orchestration. One coordinator owns one ignored feat/aws-ecs-program worktree rooted at immutable PROGRAM_BASE_SHA, serializes all mutations, closes slices on truthful program-branch anchors, and ignores unrelated concurrent documentation through Stages 1-8. Stage 9 reviews and merges the then-current release tip, preserves close ancestry, records RECONCILED_RELEASE_SHA, and runs a reconciliation checkpoint. Plan 12 Task 1 then freezes the candidate, Major Checkpoint 6 runs before Task 2, cleanup gates rereconciliation, and Task 9 fast-forwards the exact tested candidate before GO.

review_basis.head: a579626e46c97973bc50b5dc9235032ad98199d9
review_basis.process: Independent orchestration-contract, mechanical, and Wardline re-review of the uncommitted planning repair in the isolated program worktree. Checks covered worktree-only Stages 1-8, substantive documentation classification, Stage 9 conflict resume, Plan 10 replacement lineage, reconciliation versus candidate checkpoint ordering, cleanup-before-rereconciliation, source hashes, shell syntax, links, JSON, whitespace, and stale terms.

warnings.detail: Later stages still require named operator authority, Docker, hosted CI, AWS credentials and infrastructure, protected evidence storage, and cleanup capacity; absence is an explicit stop condition.
Planning approval is not AWS runtime approval. Only Plan 12 may return GO for one exact source SHA, image digest, and platform after live proof and cleanup.

resolved_findings.resolution: The exact 20-node live DAG, direct prerequisites, critical path, conservative stage barriers, and serialized shared-surface ownership are machine-checked.
The run sheet supersedes duplicate plan-local claim, worktree, branch, and close mechanics while retaining every implementation, test, evidence, staging, and commit checkbox.

Stage 9 merges the reviewed release tip into feat/aws-ecs-program and records RECONCILED_RELEASE_SHA before Task 1 freezes the candidate; Task 9 alone fast-forwards the still-anchored release branch to that exact tested SHA before GO.
Every plan is checksum-bound to its review sidecar; Plan 12 repeats that gate immediately before closeout so intentional downstream plan edits cannot consume stale approval.
Slice and integrated evidence use closed schemas requiring zero exit, zero skips, full collected-test accounting, and an envelope bound to the current shared program tip.

Fresh and interrupted work use exact-owner historical claim anchors, recover partial setup without abandoning WIP, heartbeat long leases, and close only after focused program-tip verification and anchor revalidation.

Plan 10 atomically claims before source qualification, records one idempotent qualified-baseline receipt, preserves the historical baseline through its own commits, and hard-stops post-integration lineage reconstruction without owner approval.

Reopen handling derives transitive descendants and affected phases from the refreshed live graph, reopens closed descendants in reverse topological order, invalidates their evidence, and re-executes topologically.

Stage 9 is one persistent protocol. Conflict resume reconstructs the reviewed program and release anchors from HEAD/ORIG_HEAD and MERGE_HEAD, rejects unresolved, unstaged, or untracked resolution state, and distinguishes conflict-class exit 2 from other merge failures.

A substantive reconciliation delta touching Plan 10 itself or a consumed prerequisite uses the owner-approved replacement-lineage protocol; authoritative AWS plans, reviews, designs, and specifications are all substantive.

The pre-freeze reconciliation affected-area checkpoint is distinct from Major Checkpoint 6, which runs on the Task 1 frozen candidate. After external mutation, Task 8 cleanup must finish before any HEAD change or rereconciliation.

verification: all four amended orchestration documents have balanced Markdown fences
all 75 Bash or shell fences in those documents pass bash -n
all 24 local links in those documents resolve
a synthetic divergent-release repository passed conflict-class exit, fresh-shell anchor reconstruction, staged resolution, merge commit, and preserved ancestry
targeted searches found no RELEASE_START_SHA or paused-release instruction in the amended documents
independent orchestration-contract and Wardline reviewers returned APPROVED after repair

review_history.summary: Initial orchestration reviews found duplicate claim/worktree/close mechanics, an invalid Plan 12 feature-worktree path, pre-qualification Plan 10 baseline recording, stale review/evidence risks, and incomplete descendant reopen handling.

The worktree-only Stages 1-8 contract, Stage 9 release reconciliation and conflict resume, Plan 10 special recovery, candidate-bound Major Checkpoint 6, and cleanup-before-rereconciliation ordering were independently approved.
